summary: All seven review probes pass. No Must Fix findings. One Should Fix applied (master→main in SKILL.md). One Consider deferred (scrape_changelogs.py gap for Bugs Fixed/Other Changes — pre-existing, documented in PR descriptions).

findings.title: Multiple --base master references
findings.description: Mode 1 PR create, Mode 2 master-sync PR, and Mode 3 commands used '--base master' matching the old branch name. The repo default branch is 'main'.
findings.suggestion: Update all --base master to --base main and rename 'Master sync PR' to 'Main sync PR'.
findings.changes_made: Updated all --base master to --base main in both SKILL.md files. Renamed §2.9 'Master sync PR' to 'Main sync PR'.

findings.title: scrape_changelogs.py does not extract Bugs Fixed / Other Changes / Breaking Changes subsections
findings.description: The extract_features() function only handles 'Features Added' / 'Added'. New subsection names 'Bugs Fixed', 'Other Changes', 'Breaking Changes' are not extracted. This is a pre-existing gap — the script is feature-parity focused, not full-changelog. Adding extraction of these sections would be additive but is out of scope.
findings.suggestion: Could extend extract_features() to also capture 'Bugs Fixed' / 'Other Changes' / 'Breaking Changes' as a future improvement.
findings.rationale: Pre-existing gap. The script is feature-parity focused, not full-changelog. The new 'Bugs Fixed'/'Other Changes' sections are not extracted (this was true of the old 'Fixed'/'Removed' subsections too). Documented in both PR descriptions. Patch would be <10 lines but is out of scope for this process-only work item.

findings.title: marocchino/sticky-pull-request-comment@v2 is a community action
findings.description: The action requires org allow-listing in GitHub Actions settings. If not permitted, the step will fail (non-blocking since the job is non-blocking by design, but the step itself would show as failed in the workflow run).
findings.suggestion: Document the org allow-list requirement in the PR description and in the workflow file comment. Provide fallback instructions.
findings.changes_made: PR description includes explicit warning about org allow-list requirement and fallback (inline gh pr comment with HTML marker). Spec §5.11 also documents this.

probe_results.P1_markdown_wellformed: PASS - No broken anchors, no orphan headers, no YAML-breaking characters in markdown files.
probe_results.P2_three_places_alignment: PASS - CONTRIBUTING.md, copilot-instructions.md, and changelog-check.yml all state the same four subsections (Features Added / Breaking Changes / Bugs Fixed / Other Changes) and the same classifier logic.
probe_results.P3_changelog_check_yml_validity: PASS - YAML parses cleanly. paths: filter triggers on both main and FI src; per-package missing detection is correct (MAIN_SRC vs MAIN_CL, FI_SRC vs FI_CL independent).
probe_results.P4_agent_deletion_audit: PASS - File deleted after audit. Only copilot-instructions.md referenced it; that reference has been updated to a retirement notice. File does not exist in worktree.
probe_results.P5_fi_skill_preserves_operational_detail: PASS - Partner-publish steps (§5), GA exit checklist (§2.2), hotfix branch-policy gap note (§3), pipeline links (§7), troubleshooting (§9) all preserved. Only changelog-derivation logic replaced.
probe_results.P6_prlint_yaml_folded_scalar: PASS - yaml.safe_load() returns a string (not list/dict). Title regex on line 16 unchanged. New comment text references CONTRIBUTING.md and changelog.md correctly.
probe_results.P7_pr_cross_link: PASS - Both PR descriptions reference the partner PR. A pinned comment was added to toolkit PR #68 explicitly cross-linking to dotnet-v3 PR #5864.
